nodes.context: American white supremacist and Neo-Nazi prison and street gang, one of the largest and most violent in the US.
Separate prison gang and crime syndicate founded in California in the 1960s.
Rival white supremacist prison gang formed by members who left the ABT merger.
Black prison gang that fought the ABT in its first war.
Prison gang that obtained a peace treaty with the ABT in the early 1990s.
Prison gang that was 'parked' after wars with the ABT in 1995/1996.
Mexican drug cartel listed as an ally of the ABT in the infobox.
White supremacist gang mentioned in a 2018 federal crackdown.
White supremacist gang mentioned in a 2018 federal crackdown.
White supremacist gang mentioned in a 2018 federal crackdown.

edges.evidence_quote: Despite the similarity in their names, the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas is not affiliated with the Aryan Brotherhood (AB), a separate and also notorious prison gang and crime syndicate founded in California in the 1960s with branches in the federal and state prison systems and outside prison walls.
edges.context: The text explicitly states the two groups are not affiliated.

edges.evidence_quote: The Aryan Circle was the next major war, which lasted roughly 8 years (1986–1994).
edges.context: The ABT and Aryan Circle were in a state of war from 1986 to 1994.

edges.evidence_quote: Then A.C. murdered a member of ABT in 1998 on McConnell Unit. War raged until 2009. Then, yet again, war would come to these two groups, starting in 2015 and still going.
edges.context: The ABT and Aryan Circle resumed war from 1998 to 2009, and again from 2015 onward.

edges.evidence_quote: Then, yet again, war would come to these two groups, starting in 2015 and still going.
edges.context: The ABT and Aryan Circle resumed war in 2015 and it continues.

edges.evidence_quote: The ABT's first war was with the black gang Mandingo Warriors, who were beaten to the point of near extermination within the prison system.
edges.context: The ABT fought and nearly exterminated the Mandingo Warriors.

edges.evidence_quote: the Mexican Mafia and Aryan Circle came away with 'peace treaties' from them in the early 1990s.
edges.context: The ABT agreed to a peace treaty with the Mexican Mafia in the early 1990s.

edges.evidence_quote: Texas Mafia (TM) was 'parked' system wide, meaning they were no longer allowed to recruit members, after the ABT–TM wars in 1995/1996.
edges.context: The ABT fought wars with the Texas Mafia in 1995/1996, resulting in the TM being 'parked'.
The infobox lists the Gulf Cartel as an ally of the ABT.
The infobox lists the Aryan Circle as a rival of the ABT.
